title: Greece 7% flat tax: pension eligibility checklist
intro: Greece offers foreign pensioners a flat 7 percent annual tax on foreign-source income for up to 15 years under Article 5B of the Income Tax Code. This checklist covers the five published eligibility conditions. Work through each item before consulting a Greek tax adviser. This is screening information, not tax advice.

steps.title: Prior non-residency in Greece
steps.detail: Confirm you were not a Greek tax resident for at least five of the six calendar years before the year in which you intend to transfer your tax residence to Greece.

steps.title: Transfer from a qualifying country
steps.detail: Confirm your current tax residence is in a country that has an administrative-cooperation agreement in the field of taxation with Greece. EU member states and most OECD countries qualify.

steps.title: Application deadline of 31 March
steps.detail: Submit your application for tax-residence transfer and election of this regime by 31 March of the tax year in which you transfer. Applications received after that date apply to the following tax year.

steps.title: Confirm the 7% headline rate applies to all foreign income
steps.detail: The flat rate covers all foreign-source income, replacing the standard progressive tax on that income. Greek-source income is taxed separately under standard rules.

steps.title: Understand the full-payment requirement
steps.detail: The full annual tax liability must be paid each year without exception. Non-payment in any single year terminates the regime permanently; standard Greek income-tax rules then apply to worldwide income from that year onward.
